spans.text: Call the strange spirit that abides unseen In wilds , and wastes , and shaggy solitudes , And bid his dim hand
roots Overhang the torrent's channelled course
sways its murmuring and mossy boughs Above thy head
now , when the wind at times
now , when the wind at times
that thou holdest converse with some Power Invisible and strange
the gloom Of rocks and woods
mocking the high throne of God
Thou art alone in this dark world and wide
Witching the mind with tender cozenage
the lapse Of the white waters
thy head , amid the shivered slate
the prone cascade , Airy , and light , and elegant , the birch Displays its glossy stem , amid the gloom Of alders and jagged fern , and evermore Waves her light pensile foliage
The season that shall shatter your fair leaves , Gay children of the summer ! yet enjoy Your pleasant prime
The season that shall shatter your fair leaves , Gay children of the summer ! yet enjoy Your pleasant prime
virtue which shall best Avail them , when the evil days
the brief blossoms of their springtime
wind we up the glen , and hear below The dashing torrent , in deep woods concealed , And now again white-flashing on the view , Over the huge craggy fragments
thee thus bound on from age to age Unseen of man , while awful Nature sat On the rent rocks
She saw thee thus bound on from age to age Unseen of man , while awful Nature sat On the rent rocks
Halfway up The purple heath is seen , but
it not Even so with you , poor orphans , you who climb The rugged path of life without a friend ; And over broken crags bear hardly on
a friend ; And over broken crags
it not Even so with you , poor orphans , you who climb The rugged path of life without a friend ; And over broken crags bear hardly on
He who sustains the bleating lamb shall feed And comfort you
He who sustains the bleating lamb shall feed And comfort you
through the whispering wood We steal
the old and mossy oaks Imboss the mountain slope
smile the more , and the full mind , Sliding from scenes of dread magnificence
smile the more , and the full mind , Sliding from scenes of dread magnificence
smile the more , and the full mind , Sliding from scenes of dread magnificence
smiling he returns To life's consoling sympathies

he returns To life's consoling sympathies

smile the more , and the full mind , Sliding from scenes of dread magnificence
a dream , through an enchanted land , Here , with Macbeth , in the dread cavern hails The weird sisters
surge Singing her smooth air quaintly
her dreary track Still Fancy follows , and at dead of night
her panting climbs , and with sure step Avoids the jutting fragments
sheds Its solemn grandeur on the shadowy scene
some chosen strain Of Handel gently recreate the sense
Foaming in the wild stream that winds below
pendent boughs its mossy course , And , looking
the pale minstrel , while a strange And beauteous light filled his uplifted eye , Hath ever sounded into mortal ears
not now the horse or spear
now the foeman's might ; Victory the cry shall hear Of those who for their country fight
the rude steep , let Contemplation lead Our footsteps
off To see it stretch in peace
with wood Mantled , beyond abrupt and bare
sailing over the crags , or straggling lamb Bleats for its mother
to Him Who made the world
bade the seasons roll , and they rolled on In harmony ;
we hear The noise of generations
that float Like summer-clouds before the eye , and charm The pensive wanderer's way , it's sweeter yet
that in this world a brother lives
smiles the scene , that , ' mid the wilds Of rocks and mountains , the bemused thought Remembers of humanity , and calls The wildly-roving fancy back to life
with beauty not its own The ridged mountain's tract
with rich luxuriance The pasture's lawny range
the romantic vale where Ellen winds
